item.title: Drua to host historic Super Rugby Women's Final in Ba
item.description: Drua to host historic Super Rugby Women's Final in Ba

item.title: Spain and Argentina set for blockbuster FIFA World Cup final
item.description: The stage is set for a blockbuster FIFA World Cup final tomorrow morning as Spain and defending champions Argentina battle for football's biggest prize. The final will see two of the tournament's best teams go head-to-head in what promises to be an exciting contest .
item.content:encoded:
The stage is set for a blockbuster FIFA World Cup final tomorrow morning as Spain and defending champions Argentina battle for football's biggest prize.

The final will see two of the tournament's best teams go head-to-head in what promises to be an exciting contest .

Argentina head into the final with the tournament's most dangerous attack, having scored 19 goals, while Spain boast the best defence after conceding just one goal throughout the competition.

The match will also be the first World Cup final in 60 years to feature the champions of South America and Europe.

Adding even more excitement is the battle between generations, with 19-year-old Spanish sensation Lamine Yamal set to face his childhood hero, 39-year-old Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who is chasing a second World Cup title.

Messi will also be looking to end the tournament on a high as he tries to catch France's Kylian Mbappe in the race for the Golden Boot.

Meanwhile, football fans can also look forward to one of the longest halftime shows in FIFA World Cup history, featuring performances from international music stars before the second half gets underway.

In the FIFA World Cup final, Spain will take on Argentina at 7am tomorrow.

item.title: Rewa thrash Nadroga 6-0 in Extra Premier League
item.description: Rewa FC produced a dominant performance to thrash Nadroga FC 6-0 in their Extra Premier League clash today. Josaia Sela opened the scoring in the 28th minute before Asivorosi Rabo doubled Rewa's lead three minutes later to give the hosts a 2-0 advantage at halftime.
item.content:encoded:
Rewa FC produced a dominant performance to thrash Nadroga FC 6-0 in their Extra Premier League clash today.

Josaia Sela opened the scoring in the 28th minute before Asivorosi Rabo doubled Rewa's lead three minutes later to give the hosts a 2-0 advantage at halftime.

Don Keana converted a penalty a minute into the second half to make it 3-0 before grabbing his second goal in the 65th minute. Epeli Valevou added the fifth goal in the 85th minute, while Zafir Ali completed the rout with a goal in the 88th minute.

In other matches, Ba edged Navua 1-0, Nasinu came from behind to defeat Lautoka 2-1, while Tailevu/Naitasiri secured all three points with a narrow 1-0 win over Nadi.

item.title: 13 year old aspiring referee's dream comes true at Eden Park
item.description: Vaibhavi, who hopes to one day officiate at the highest level of world rugby, was in Auckland with her father, Dhanjay Deo, to watch the All Blacks take on Ireland when she received an unexpected surprise. Just before kick-off, she was invited from her seat to the sideline, where she joined the international match officials as they completed their final preparations for the Test.
item.content:encoded:
Vaibhavi, who hopes to one day officiate at the highest level of world rugby, was in Auckland with her father, Dhanjay Deo, to watch the All Blacks take on Ireland when she received an unexpected surprise.

Just before kick-off, she was invited from her seat to the sideline, where she joined the international match officials as they completed their final preparations for the Test.

From ground level, Vaibhavi watched the teams run onto the field, witnessed the national anthems and the famous All Blacks haka, and spent the match learning how elite referees prepare, communicate and work together.

Speaking after the experience, Vaibhavi says she had no idea what was happening when she was taken from her seat and described it as the biggest surprise of her life.

She says seeing how international referees prepare for a Test match and watching the haka from the sideline, has inspired her to work even harder towards her goal of one day returning to Eden Park as a referee.

After the match, Australian referee Nic Berry presented Vaibhavi with a signed World Rugby match official's jersey, to encourage her to continue pursuing her dream.

Rugby Australia has also expressed their interest in supporting her development as she works towards becoming an international referee.

Her father, Dhanjay Deo, says the trip was originally planned so they could watch their first All Blacks Test together, but the surprise made it an experience the family will never forget.

Vaibhavi's journey has also been supported by Fiji Airways, while the Fiji Rugby Union and the Suva Rugby Referees Association continue to mentor and encourage her as she pursues her dream of becoming a top-level rugby referee.

item.title: Mbappe edges closer to second Golden Boot after France defeat
item.description: Kylian Mbappe is on the verge of winning a second FIFA World Cup Golden Boot after scoring twice in France's thrilling 6-4 defeat to England in the third-place play-off. The two goals took Mbappe's tournament tally to 10, putting him two goals ahead of Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who has one last chance to catch him in tomorrow morning's World Cup final against Spain.
item.content:encoded:
Kylian Mbappe is on the verge of winning a second FIFA World Cup Golden Boot after scoring twice in France's thrilling 6-4 defeat to England in the third-place play-off.

The two goals took Mbappe's tournament tally to 10, putting him two goals ahead of Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who has one last chance to catch him in tomorrow morning's World Cup final against Spain.

Messi must score twice to draw level with Mbappe and would win the Golden Boot on assists. If he scores once or fails to score, Mbappe will become the first player to win the award twice.

Mbappe also leads the FIFA World Cup all-time scoring chart with 22 goals, one ahead of Messi.

Meanwhile, England's Jude Bellingham is third in the Golden Boot race with seven goals, ahead of Norway's Erling Haaland on assists. England captain Harry Kane and France's Ousmane Dembele are tied on six goals.

Meanwhile, in the FIFA World Cup final, Spain will take on Argentina at 7am tomorrow.

item.title: England beat France 6-4 in thrilling World Cup third-place play-off
item.description: England survived a fierce second-half comeback from France to claim third place at the FIFA World Cup with a thrilling 6-4 victory. After storming to a 4-0 halftime lead, England were pushed all the way as Kylian Mbappé scored twice to become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading goalscorer and move to the top of the Golden Boot race.
item.content:encoded:
England survived a fierce second-half comeback from France to claim third place at the FIFA World Cup with a thrilling 6-4 victory.

After storming to a 4-0 halftime lead, England were pushed all the way as Kylian Mbappé scored twice to become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading goalscorer and move to the top of the Golden Boot race.

Declan Rice got England off to the perfect start in the third minute before Ezri Konsa doubled the lead 15 minutes later.

Bukayo Saka then took centre stage, scoring twice before halftime to leave England in complete control at the break.

France came out firing in the second half as Mbappé struck in the 48th and 66th minutes, with Bradley Barcola scoring in between to cut the deficit to just one goal.

Saka completed his hat-trick from the penalty spot in the 87th minute to make it 5-3, but Ousmane Dembélé pulled one back for France in stoppage time to set up a tense finish.

With France chasing an equaliser, Jude Bellingham struck in the eighth minute of added time to wrap up a pulsating 6-4 victory for England.

Meanwhile, in the FIFA World Cup final, Spain will take on Argentina at 7am tomorrow.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys top Group A after 3-0 win over PNG
item.description: The Junior Bula Boys have topped Group A at the OFC Under-16 Men's Championship after beating hosts Papua New Guinea 3-0. Fiji took a 3-0 lead into halftime and held on to the advantage until fulltime to secure all three points and finish at the top of the standings.
item.content:encoded:
The Junior Bula Boys have topped Group A at the OFC Under-16 Men's Championship after beating hosts Papua New Guinea 3-0.

Fiji took a 3-0 lead into halftime and held on to the advantage until fulltime to secure all three points and finish at the top of the standings.

Following the win, the team expressed their gratitude to their supporters and families back in Fiji for their continued support throughout the tournament.

The victory keeps Fiji's unbeaten run intact as they progress to the knockout stage of the competition.

The Junior Bula Boys will now face the Group B runners-up in the semi-finals on Wednesday at 4pm at PNG Football Stadium.

item.title: Bulldogs crush Tigers 32-0
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/07/2026 The Bulldogs demolished the Wests Tigers 32-0 in the NRL last night.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/07/2026

The Bulldogs demolished the Wests Tigers 32-0 in the NRL last night.

With Viliame Kikau back from a pectoral injury and Sean O'Sullivan starring in the halves, the Bulldogs racked up their eighth win of the season to climb to 11th on the ladder ahead of a blockbuster clash with the Warriors in Round 21.

20-year-old Jethro Rinakama scored the Bulldogs' first try, followed by Josh Curran and Enari Tuala, to give Canterbury an 18-0 halftime lead.

Matt Burton extended Canterbury's lead in the 65th minute, and Curran crossed for his second try in the 72nd minute to seal the victory.

Burton was flawless with the boot, converting all five tries and adding a penalty goal.

In other results, the Raiders defeated the Rabbitohs 32-24, while the Warriors beat the Dragons 20-12.

Looking at today's matches, the Titans will take on the Sea Eagles at 4pm, before the Dolphins meet the Cowboys at 6.05pm.

item.title: World Cup rings for winners as Trump attendance confirmed
item.description: FIFA will present World Cup rings to the winners of Monday's final between Argentina and Spain, which will be attended by US Presi
